_comment: Client DNA — Fern Private Care brand identity. Healthcare premium. Human, warm, trustworthy.

brand_strategy.positioning: La agencia de cuidado personal más confiable de Florida — cuidadores certificados que tratan a tus seres queridos como propios
brand_strategy.tone_of_voice: Speaks to adult children worried about their aging parents. Empathetic, reassuring, professional. Never clinical or cold. Uses 'your loved one', not 'the patient'.
brand_strategy.unique_angle: Personal care that feels like family — professional standards with human warmth. 24/7 availability, background-checked caregivers, flexible in-home or facility options.

visual_identity.feel: Warm, human, deeply trustworthy — like a family member who happens to be a healthcare professional. Generous white space, soft teal tones, real photography of caregivers with seniors.

design_direction.hero_concept: Split layout: left = powerful headline addressing the family's fear + key trust signals + CTA | right = warm real photo of caregiver with elderly person, genuine interaction
design_direction.color_approach: White base with sections alternating white / soft teal-tinted gray / one warm dark accent section. Teal as primary for buttons and highlights. Amber for secondary CTAs.
design_direction.visual_break_concept: Dark teal band with 4 key trust metrics (10+ years, 24/7 availability, background-checked, 100% personalized) — displayed large, centered
design_direction.typography_personality: Sans-serif (Inter). Headlines bold (font-extrabold). Warm, readable, generous line height. Never tight or aggressive.
design_direction.section_spacing: spacious — plenty of breathing room, never cramped
forbidden_patterns: Generic stock photos of nurses in white uniforms looking at cameras
Blue/purple color scheme (this is not a tech product)
Aggressive pricing or limited-time offer language
Dense text blocks or walls of copy
Dark backgrounds for most sections (only the trust-metrics band goes dark)
Testimonials as 3 identical white cards on a colored gradient background
CTA buttons that say only 'Learn More' with no benefit
Section backgrounds all the same (alternation is mandatory)
Photos of empty hospital rooms or sterile medical equipment

business_goals.primary: leads — get the family to book a free consultation or call
business_goals.key_objection: How do I know I can trust a stranger with my loved one?
business_goals.conversion_barrier: Fear and guilt — the family feels guilty for not doing this themselves
business_goals.conversion_trigger: Seeing real caregivers + hearing from other families who made this decision + 24/7 availability removing the fear of emergencies

key_sections.priority_section: testimonials — families trust other families more than any marketing claim

reference_inspiration.what_to_take: Generous spacing, real photography, trust signals prominently displayed, warm color palette, emotional copy that addresses the reader's fear first
reference_inspiration.what_to_improve: Add concrete caregiver credentials, 24/7 reassurance messaging throughout, process section showing how easy it is to get started
reference_inspiration.avoid: Anything that feels like a hospital brochure or insurance document
